import pytest
from source import filter_expensive_items

def test_type_coercion_price():
    items = [
        {'name': 'A', 'price': 100},
        {'name': 'B', 'price': '200'},
        {'name': 'C', 'price': 150},
        {'name': 'D', 'price': '150'}
    ]
    threshold = 150
    # Should filter items with price > 150, converting string prices to int
    expected = [
        {'name': 'B', 'price': '200'},
        {'name': 'C', 'price': 150},  # 150 is not > 150, so this should not be included
    ]
    # Actually, let's correct: only items with price > 150
    expected = [
        {'name': 'B', 'price': '200'},
    ]
    
    # The buggy version will raise TypeError when comparing '200' > 150
    result = filter_expensive_items(items, threshold)
    
    # Verify the result matches expected (only item B with price '200')
    assert result == expected, f"Expected {expected}, got {result}"
